name: mega_burst
description: Saturates the m_workersQueue producer (unlimited). 1000 concurrent agents with small data_size generate enqueue rate > combined worker capacity (std::thread::hardware_concurrency()). The goal is to expose whether the unbounded queue causes RSS to grow without limit.
notes: Hypothesis: if m_workersQueue grows unbounded, RSS climbs in a linear ramp (not sawtooth) and never stabilizes.
Key proxy: monitor_rss_mb.png — look for a sustained positive slope during the send window.
Compare with burst.json (200 agents): if RSS stayed flat in burst but rises monotonically in mega_burst, the queue is responsible.
If the host can't reach 1000 agents due to ulimit/fds, lower to 500 and increase data_size — the goal is input_rate > worker_drain_rate.
Mitigation to evaluate after the experiment: max capacity for m_workersQueue + drop_oldest/reject policy with logging.
